texts.label: paragraph
texts.orig: DAC TECHNOLOGIES & CAPTURE CAPTURE
texts.text: DAC TECHNOLOGIES & CAPTURE CAPTURE

texts.label: paragraph
texts.orig: Two main technologies : absorption in a solvent or adsorption. Auxilary phase regeneration is needed  (on a space or time basis) and usually corresponds to the main energy requirement contribution.
texts.text: Two main technologies : absorption in a solvent or adsorption. Auxilary phase regeneration is needed  (on a space or time basis) and usually corresponds to the main energy requirement contribution.

texts.label: paragraph
texts.orig: A very large portfolio of process possibilities, depending on auxilary phase (tailor made solvents or adsorbents) and regeneration strategy (heat, pressure, electricity), among others…
texts.text: A very large portfolio of process possibilities, depending on auxilary phase (tailor made solvents or adsorbents) and regeneration strategy (heat, pressure, electricity), among others…

texts.label: paragraph
texts.orig: DAC Technologies : State of the art
texts.text: DAC Technologies : State of the art

texts.label: paragraph
texts.orig: Overall energy requirement is key for OPEX
texts.text: Overall energy requirement is key for OPEX

texts.label: paragraph
texts.orig: Productivity is key for CAPEX (installation size + footprint)
texts.text: Productivity is key for CAPEX (installation size + footprint)

texts.label: paragraph
texts.orig: Process stability, environmental impact (waste products, emissions, water footprint) should also be carefully considered
texts.text: Process stability, environmental impact (waste products, emissions, water footprint) should also be carefully considered

texts.label: paragraph
texts.orig: Key questions to be addressed for the expert review
texts.text: Key questions to be addressed for the expert review

texts.label: paragraph
texts.orig: NB: It is important to stress that the review is limited to Maïa carbon capture process, not to the regeneration part
texts.text: NB: It is important to stress that the review is limited to Maïa carbon capture process, not to the regeneration part

texts.label: paragraph
texts.orig: CO2 capture from air is classically achieved by absorption in a solvent (e.g. Carbon Engineering) or adsorption on a solid (e.g. Climeworks)
texts.text: CO2 capture from air is classically achieved by absorption in a solvent (e.g. Carbon Engineering) or adsorption on a solid (e.g. Climeworks)

texts.label: paragraph
texts.orig: For gas liquid-absorption, a chemically reactive solvent is required, due to the limited solubility of CO2 in physical solvents for low partial pressure feed streams
texts.text: For gas liquid-absorption, a chemically reactive solvent is required, due to the limited solubility of CO2 in physical solvents for low partial pressure feed streams

texts.label: paragraph
texts.orig: Classically aqueous solutions of amine solvents, (MEA, MDEA), carbonate salts, caustic soda, potassium hydroxide or amino acids show adequate performances (selectivity, capacity, kinetics, regeneration, stability, cost)
texts.text: Classically aqueous solutions of amine solvents, (MEA, MDEA), carbonate salts, caustic soda, potassium hydroxide or amino acids show adequate performances (selectivity, capacity, kinetics, regeneration, stability, cost)

texts.label: paragraph
texts.orig: Maia process makes use of KOH, a strong base which is a commodity chemical and shows a very fast kinetics. This option is relevant for carbon capture purpose. The main issue is the energy of regeneration, given the high entalphy of the KOH/CO2 system.
texts.text: Maia process makes use of KOH, a strong base which is a commodity chemical and shows a very fast kinetics. This option is relevant for carbon capture purpose. The main issue is the energy of regeneration, given the high entalphy of the KOH/CO2 system.

texts.label: paragraph
texts.orig: MAIA CO2 Capture Technology: Absorption
texts.text: MAIA CO2 Capture Technology: Absorption

texts.label: paragraph
texts.orig: KOH – 94 kJ/mol
texts.text: KOH – 94 kJ/mol

texts.label: paragraph
texts.orig: PROCESS DESIGN ANALYSIS
texts.text: PROCESS DESIGN ANALYSIS

texts.label: paragraph
texts.orig: Gas absorption in a column is a mature technology with a proven design methodology, including with chemical solvents
texts.text: Gas absorption in a column is a mature technology with a proven design methodology, including with chemical solvents

texts.label: paragraph
texts.orig: Absorption of CO2 in KOH is well documented and key design data are available for this system
texts.text: Absorption of CO2 in KOH is well documented and key design data are available for this system

texts.label: paragraph
texts.orig: Maia process design study is based on the following assumptions:
texts.text: Maia process design study is based on the following assumptions:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Steady state process
texts.text: Steady state process

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Cross current operation
texts.text: Cross current operation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Isothermal conditions
texts.text: Isothermal conditions

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Water saturation for gas exhaust
texts.text: Water saturation for gas exhaust

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1D mass and energy balances
texts.text: 1D mass and energy balances

texts.label: paragraph
texts.orig: This set of assumptions is relevant and correspond to the current state of the art for gas absorption systems
texts.text: This set of assumptions is relevant and correspond to the current state of the art for gas absorption systems

texts.label: paragraph
texts.orig: Carbon capture absorption unit:  design by Maia
texts.text: Carbon capture absorption unit:  design by Maia

texts.label: paragraph
texts.orig: The liquid over gas flowrate ratio (L/G) is around 1 on a mass basis
texts.text: The liquid over gas flowrate ratio (L/G) is around 1 on a mass basis

texts.label: paragraph
texts.orig: CO2 solubility is evaluated through Henry’s law (physical solubility) and chemical solubility through stoechiometry
texts.text: CO2 solubility is evaluated through Henry’s law (physical solubility) and chemical solubility through stoechiometry

texts.label: paragraph
texts.orig: Interfacial area (Brentwood Industries XF12560 packing), film thickness and liquid mass transfer coefficient are typical of design guidelines for packed columns. The effective mass transfer indicator K.a is around 0.3 s-1 which is on the upper range of DAC carbon capture technologies
texts.text: Interfacial area (Brentwood Industries XF12560 packing), film thickness and liquid mass transfer coefficient are typical of design guidelines for packed columns. The effective mass transfer indicator K.a is around 0.3 s-1 which is on the upper range of DAC carbon capture technologies

texts.label: paragraph
texts.orig: The CO2 capture ratio is fixed at 70% which is often considered as adequate for DAC
texts.text: The CO2 capture ratio is fixed at 70% which is often considered as adequate for DAC

texts.label: paragraph
texts.orig: WARNINGS
texts.text: WARNINGS

texts.label: paragraph
texts.orig: Column pressure drop is important: a 1 mBar limit is often suggested in order to decrease the energy requirement  of the capture step
texts.text: Column pressure drop is important: a 1 mBar limit is often suggested in order to decrease the energy requirement  of the capture step

texts.label: paragraph
texts.orig: Water losses through evaporation can be important and strongly depend on inlet temperature and humidity
texts.text: Water losses through evaporation can be important and strongly depend on inlet temperature and humidity

texts.label: paragraph
texts.orig: Maia carbon capture absorption unit
texts.text: Maia carbon capture absorption unit

texts.label: paragraph
texts.orig: Water losses as a function of T and RH for KOH absorption
texts.text: Water losses as a function of T and RH for KOH absorption

texts.label: paragraph
texts.orig: SYNTHESIS
texts.text: SYNTHESIS

texts.label: paragraph
texts.orig: Maia choice of a gas liquid absorption process is relevant for DAC carbon capture step.
texts.text: Maia choice of a gas liquid absorption process is relevant for DAC carbon capture step.

texts.label: paragraph
texts.orig: KOH is a very effective solvent (e.g. selected by Carbon Engineering) showing availability, stability and very high absorption kinetics. The main impact is the regeneration energy requirement (not considered in the present analysis).
texts.text: KOH is a very effective solvent (e.g. selected by Carbon Engineering) showing availability, stability and very high absorption kinetics. The main impact is the regeneration energy requirement (not considered in the present analysis).

texts.label: paragraph
texts.orig: The air contactor design calculation performed by Maia makes use of a robust, proven methodology taking advantage of a strong feedback on packed column for CO2 absorption in KOH. The set of assumptions, overall resolution methodology and final design data are liable.
texts.text: The air contactor design calculation performed by Maia makes use of a robust, proven methodology taking advantage of a strong feedback on packed column for CO2 absorption in KOH. The set of assumptions, overall resolution methodology and final design data are liable.

texts.label: paragraph
texts.orig: Column pressure drop and water losses through evaporation should be more carefully taken into account. Energy requirement, productivity and water losses of Maïa capture process should be systematically computed for different set op conditions in order to enabe a comparison to alternative technologies.
texts.text: Column pressure drop and water losses through evaporation should be more carefully taken into account. Energy requirement, productivity and water losses of Maïa capture process should be systematically computed for different set op conditions in order to enabe a comparison to alternative technologies.

texts.label: paragraph
texts.orig: Given the rigorness of the pre-design study, the next step should be testing a carbon capture pilot unit based on the reported process geometry and operating conditions
texts.text: Given the rigorness of the pre-design study, the next step should be testing a carbon capture pilot unit based on the reported process geometry and operating conditions
